title: Concorda logigraph ontology node
description: One node = one named concept in the system: a role (system or relational), a resource (Boat, EventCrew), an action (Accept, Promote), or an attribute (resume_published, status). Rules reference these nodes by id.

schema_version.description: Logigraph nodes are schema_version: 2 to distinguish from depgraph (schema_version: 1).

id.description: Form: <subkind>::<category>::<short_name>. Example: role::relational::boat_owner. Stable; renames are explicit migrations.

title.description: Human-readable label for the concept. E.g. "Boat Owner", "EventCrew", "Accept".

summary.description: One- or two-sentence plain-English description. Used for inline injection at edit time. Full structured definition lives in the dossier.

source.description: Where this concept comes from. For deterministically-extracted nodes (system roles, DB resources), points at the file/line. For authored relational roles and abstract concepts, names the predicate or describes the conceptual basis.

aliases.description: Alternative names this concept is known by. E.g. role::system::org_admin has alias 'org_admin' (the bare DB string).

definition_status.description: stub: extractor placeholder. llm_drafted: Claude wrote the dossier. human_reviewed: Logan signed off. Hooks skip stubs with a banner.

structural_hash.description: SHA-256 over canonical fields (id + summary + source + aliases). Bumping this signals dossier may need re-review.

dossier.description: Path to the plain-language dossier markdown (relative to logigraph/).

extractor.description: Which extractor produced this node, if any. Null for hand-authored concepts.
